appDesc.message: See more accurate Google Maps ratings with estimates adjusted for reviews removed as “defamation”.
appDesc.description: Extension description (must stay under 132 characters for the Web Store)

badgeCaption.description: Caption in the badge bar (deliberately says 'estimate', not 'rating')

badgeTooltipWorst.message: Google reports that $REMOVED$ review(s) were removed from this profile due to defamation complaints. This worst-case estimate assumes the top of that range and treats each removed review as a rating of $ASSUMED$. The average could then drop to $CORRECTED$ instead of $OFFICIAL$. The actual removed ratings are unknown, so this is an estimate and not a verified rating.

badgeTooltipConservative.message: Google reports that $REMOVED$ review(s) were removed from this profile due to defamation complaints. This conservative estimate assumes the bottom of that range and treats each removed review as a rating of $ASSUMED$. The average would then be $CORRECTED$ instead of $OFFICIAL$. The actual removed ratings are unknown, so this is an estimate and not a verified rating.

checkingText.description: Neutral state while the Reviews tab is probed

cleanCaption.message: No defamation-based review removals reported by Google — respect for letting honest feedback stand.

popupCalcHint.message: Both modes use the removed-review star value set below. Worst case takes the highest number in Google's reported range, conservative takes the lowest.

popupRemovedValueHint.message: 1 star is the harshest assumption. Higher values soften the estimate, because the actual star ratings of removed reviews are unknown.

popupWorstCaseHint.message: Upper bound assumed when Google only reports that over 250 reviews were removed.

popupMethodText.message: Google Maps shows when reviews were removed from a business profile after defamation complaints, but only as a range (e.g. “2 to 5”). ReviewShield recalculates the average by treating every removed review as having the star value you set, using either the top of the range (worst case) or the bottom (conservative). The actual removed ratings are unknown, so the result is an estimate. Profiles without reported removals get a clean bill instead. Everything runs locally in your browser: no external requests, no analytics, no data collection.
